title: Conflict UX Design for Section-Level Sync

slides.heading: Conflict UX Design
slides.subtitle: Four surfaces for section-level sync conflict resolution

slides.heading: The Problem
slides.body:
- `sherpa sync` merges markdown convention files at **section** (heading) granularity
- When both upstream and consumer modify the same section, what does the user see?
- No existing tool does section-level conflict resolution for markdown
- Spans CLI, editor integration, and web UI

slides.heading: Key Research Findings
slides.body:
- **Inline markers won.** Copier switched default from .rej to inline (v8.0.0). Cruft users actively downgrade to get inline back
- **Batch-first is validated.** Copier deliberately rejected interactive resolution 3 times over 10 years
- **Git-compatible = free tooling.** VS Code shows Accept/Reject buttons for any file with `<<<<<<<` markers
- **Deferred resolution is essential.** Convention file conflicts don't block development (Jujutsu pattern)
- **Editor integration is viable.** `code --wait --merge` works standalone without git (chezmoi proves it)

left.body:
**Design principles:**
- Git-compatible delimiters
- Section title on every marker line
- diff3-style (includes base)
- Machine-readable HTML comment header
- `grep '^<<<<<<< '` = conflict TOC

right.body:
```
<!-- sherpa:conflict sections=1 -->
<<<<<<< upstream — ## Config (modified)
timeout: 60s
||||||| base — ## Config
timeout: 30s
======= consumer — ## Config (modified)
timeout: 45s
>>>>>>> end — ## Config
```

slides.heading: Four-Surface Escalation Cascade
slides.body:
| Surface | Tech | Phase | When |
|---------|------|-------|------|
| **1. Batch markers** | Git-compatible + section metadata | MVP | Always (default output) |
| **2. Interactive CLI** | Ink + `git add -p` model | MVP | `--interactive` or TTY |
| **3. Editor merge** | `code --merge` (configurable) | Phase 2 | Complex sections |
| **4. Studio UI** | @codemirror/merge + shadcn | Phase 2 | Visual merge panel |

left.body:
**Framework:** Ink (React for CLIs)
- Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Wrangler all use Ink
- `useInput` for single-key actions
- `useFocus` for section navigation
- Progress footer with keybinding help

right.body:
**Keybindings** (git add -p model):
- `a` — Accept upstream
- `k` — Keep mine
- `e` — Edit in $EDITOR
- `d` — Defer (resolve later)
- `A`/`K` — All remaining
- `q` — Quit (write markers)

slides.heading: Studio Merge UI Stack
slides.body:
- **Diff algorithm:** node-diff3 — only mature JS three-way merge library
- **Merge view:** @codemirror/merge — built-in acceptChunk/rejectChunk APIs
- **Custom controls:** mergeControls renderer for shadcn-styled buttons
- **Layout:** shadcn ResizablePanelGroup + Tabs + ScrollArea
- **Not Monaco:** DiffEditor is display-only, 3-way merge editor is internal

slides.heading: Open Questions for Next Iteration
slides.body:
1. **Conflict persistence** — `.sherpa-conflicts.json` schema for defer/resume
2. **CI/automation mode** — exit codes, machine-readable reports, pipeline safety
3. **Section ownership interaction** — how `<!-- sherpa:managed -->` affects conflict UX
4. **Ink component architecture** — `<SectionDiff>`, scrolling, $EDITOR suspend/resume
5. **Studio ↔ sync engine** — MCP real-time vs file-based integration
